analysis_result: 77 samples are associated with NF1 syndrome (NF1 Status = 'NF1').
denominator_used: N = 108; definition: all rows in MOESM4 'Clinical Data' sheet with non-null Tumor_ID.
assumptions_made: Filtered to rows with non-null Tumor_ID as directed by data context; treated exact string match 'NF1' in the 'NF1 Status' column as the definition of NF1-syndrome-associated samples; the only other observed value was 'Sporadic' (31 samples), with no missing values.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
- File: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx, sheet 'Clinical Data'
- Shape: 108 rows x 15 columns
- Relevant columns: Tumor_ID, NF1 Status

B. COHORT DEFINITION
- Filter: Tumor_ID non-null -> 108 samples remaining (no rows dropped; all 108 rows have non-null Tumor_ID)
- Final analytic cohort: 108 samples

C. VARIABLE CODING
- Column: 'NF1 Status'
- Value distribution: NF1 = 77, Sporadic = 31, NaN = 0
- Samples associated with NF1 syndrome defined as NF1 Status == 'NF1'

D. COMPUTATION
- Numerator: rows where NF1 Status == 'NF1' = 77
- Denominator: 108 (all samples with non-null Tumor_ID)
- Operation: (df['NF1 Status'] == 'NF1').sum()

E. RESULT
- 77 samples are NF1-syndrome-associated (77/108 = 71.3%)

F. CAVEATS
- Relies on the NF1 Status column being coded consistently; only two values observed ('NF1' and 'Sporadic'); no missing values were present so no imputation/handling needed.
